examples.title: Using execl() to execute ls command
examples.description: Example showing how to use execl to replace current program with ls -l
examples.explanation: execl takes arguments as a list (variable arguments). First argument is full path, subsequent are arguments, last must be NULL. If successful, child is replaced by ls -l.

examples.title: Using execvp() to execute command with PATH search
examples.description: Example showing execvp which searches PATH for the executable
examples.explanation: execvp takes executable name and array of arguments. It searches PATH for the executable. The array must be NULL-terminated.

examples.title: Parent-Child Communication using Pipe
examples.description: Demonstrates bidirectional pipe communication between parent and child
examples.explanation: Two pipes needed for bidirectional communication. Pipe1 for parent-to-child, pipe2 for child-to-parent. Close unused ends in each process.

examples.title: Creating Named Pipe (FIFO)
examples.description: Shows how to create and use a named pipe (FIFO) for IPC
examples.explanation: FIFO created with mkfifo(). Opens as regular file. Multiple unrelated processes can open - one for reading, one for writing. unlink removes FIFO.

examples.title: System V Shared Memory with Semaphore
examples.description: Demonstrates shared memory creation and semaphore synchronization
examples.explanation: shmget creates shared memory, shmat attaches it. Semaphore (semop) protects critical section. wait() decrements (locks), signal() increments (unlocks).

examples.title: Using execve() with custom environment
examples.description: Shows how execve() allows custom environment for new program
examples.explanation: execve() takes three arguments: pathname, argument array, and environment array. This replaces the process environment completely with custom variables.
